title: Urban Bioremediation Reactor
description: The user operates a city-scale bioremediation system where iridescent abalone-shell catalysts and bioengineered plants purify polluted urban water. Players must balance reagent concentrations, catalyst placement, and plant absorption rates across multiple reactor stages while obeying conservation of mass, with full undo/redo support for each action.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 that implements an interactive urban bioremediation chemistry simulation. Load VirtualCity.glb as the central scene representing a polluted industrial district. Place 4 DiffuseTransmissionPlant.glb instances at cardinal positions around the city as bio-filter stations. Place 3 IridescenceAbalone.glb instances as iridescent catalyst nodes that the user can drag between stations. Camera: OrbitControls, initial perspective at 45 degrees looking down at the city. Lighting: one DirectionalLight with shadows, one HemisphereLight for ambient, and PointLights on each active catalyst that glow based on reaction intensity. Interaction system (10-14 steps): The user manages 3 chemical pollutants (NOx, SOx, heavy metals) shown as colored particle clouds over the city. Each plant station has sliders for water flow rate and pH level. Dragging an abalone catalyst onto a plant station activates a catalytic reaction visualized by the abalone shell glowing and the plant's translucency pulsing. Conservation law: total pollutant mass is conserved across all stations; reducing one pollutant at a station increases byproduct concentration elsewhere unless balanced. Multi-input coupling: reaction efficiency depends on the combination of pH, flow rate, AND which catalyst is present. Display a HUD with pollutant levels per station, total system mass balance bar, and a reaction log. Implement full undo/redo stack (Ctrl+Z/Ctrl+Y) that reverts slider changes, catalyst placements, and flow adjustments. Goal state: reduce all three pollutants below threshold (green indicator) while keeping mass balance within 2% tolerance. Use raycasting for drag-and-drop of abalone catalysts. Animate plant glow intensity proportional to absorption rate using shader material emissive property. Show particle systems for pollutant clouds that shrink or grow based on current concentrations. Include a reset button and step counter.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  totalPollutantMass: number,         // total conserved mass of all pollutants in the system (NOx + SOx + heavy metals)
  noxConcentration: number,         // current NOx pollutant concentration in ppm
  soxConcentration: number,         // current SOx pollutant concentration in ppm
  heavyMetalConcentration: number,         // current heavy metal pollutant concentration in ppm
  activeCatalystCount: number,         // number of abalone catalysts currently placed on plant stations
  totalWaterFlowRate: number,         // sum of water flow rates across all 4 plant stations in L/min
  averageSystemPH: number,         // average pH level across all active plant stations
  reactionIntensity: number,         // current catalytic reaction intensity (0-100 scale)
  byproductAccumulation: number,         // accumulated byproduct mass from unbalanced reactions
  plantAbsorptionRate: number,         // total pollutant absorption rate by all plants in mg/s
  catalystEfficiency: number,         // average efficiency of placed catalysts (0-1 scale)
  systemStabilized: boolean,         // true when pollutant levels are below threshold and balanced
}
